overview.description: Nonprofit organization upskilling women in digital, IT, cybersecurity and AI skills across Czechia
overview.description_long: Czechitas (z.ú.) is a Czech nonprofit founded in 2014 that teaches digital and IT skills — including web development, data analysis, AI, and cybersecurity — primarily to women. With 76,400+ course participants and 1,900+ IT courses delivered, it is the largest women-in-tech community in the Czech Republic. Courses run across Prague and Brno in evening and weekend formats, supported by a volunteer community of 1,000+ lecturers, coaches and mentors. The organization also runs corporate upskilling programs for partner companies.
overview.tagline: Women at the heart of technological change. Upskilling and Reskilling Women in IT, Cybersecurity and AI.

history.origin_story: Founded by Dita Formánková, an IT student at Masaryk University in Brno, who noticed the stark underrepresentation of women in her field. Started as weekend Rails Girls programming workshops in Brno and quickly grew into a nationwide initiative.

key_milestones.event: Founded in Brno as Rails Girls workshops
SXSW Community Service Award; European Digital Skills Awards Finalist
European Digital Skills Awards 2024; first local verification partner in CZ/SK; 10th anniversary and Vision 2030 defined

open_roles.title: Product / Business Development for Corporate Education

awards: First local verification partner in CZ/SK 2024

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Czechitas is a mission-driven nonprofit focused on human education and community building. Its core value is a trusted learning environment, human mentorship, and social inclusion — not AI-replaceable services.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: As a nonprofit initiative addressing a societal need (gender gap in tech), Czechitas operates in a grant- and CSR-funded model rather than a competitive commercial market.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Czechitas depends on corporate sponsors (Kyndryl, Accenture, Cisco, Google.org, IBM, SAP, etc.) and grants rather than earned revenue. This creates volatility risk if major sponsors reduce CSR budgets, though its long track record (12 years) and award recognition provide stability.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: As a well-established nonprofit with 12 years of operation, 76,400+ course participants, 22+ awards, and support from major tech companies, Czechitas has a strong community and reputational moat. Funding risk is real but mitigated by diversified donor base and growing demand for tech upskilling.

metadata.notes: Czechitas is a registered nonprofit (z.ú. = zapsaný ústav), IČO 22834958, address: náměstí Bratří Synků 1748/17, Praha 4 - Nusle. Has a sub-organization Nadační fond Czechitas (foundation). Volunteer teaching community of 1,000+ is separate from paid staff. Employee count estimated from organizational size and structure.
